_comment: Master resume — JSON Resume v1 schema. Roles 2-5 populated from personal/resumes/2024-09-rishabh-sharma-sde3.pdf. Role 1 (Confido) has placeholder highlights since user is filling those manually. Per-role variants will live in resumes/variants/.

basics.name: Rishabh Sharma
basics.label: Founding / Staff Software Engineer — Healthcare AI & Voice
basics.summary: Founding software engineer with ~10 years building production systems end-to-end. Took a Series A healthcare voice-AI product from 0→1 — architecting the EHR-agnostic patient-access platform that powers AI agents across 90+ clinics and 40+ EHR systems. Known for shipping production LLM and real-time voice systems in HIPAA-regulated settings, and for turning multi-vendor integration into a 2-4 day commodity instead of a multi-week project. Open to remote-from-India and US/EU relocation.

work.name: Confido Health
work.summary: Founding engineer at a Series A ($10M) healthcare voice-AI company automating clinic front-office operations (1M+ patients enabled, >80% call automation). Architected the EHR-agnostic patient-access platform behind the voice agents. Primary cloud: GCP; HIPAA-regulated.
highlights: Architected the EHR-agnostic patient-access / clinical-workflow platform from day one — the data + action layer every voice-AI agent and forward-deployed engineer (FDE) calls to run the full front-office workload against a clinic's systems: scheduling, patient identity + demographics, insurance eligibility + verification + prior-auth, payments / balance collection (RCM), medication & prescription refills, patient documents + clinical timeline + notes retrieval, and EHR read/write sync with warm-transfer / human-in-the-loop fallback. Spans 40+ live EHR / practice-management systems; scaled from 3 pilot clinics to 90+ live clinics in ~18 months.

Built the pluggable EHR factory pattern (NestJS) + an isolated browser-automation service (Puppeteer) for legacy EHRs without API auth — cut new-EHR integration from ~10-15 days to 2-4 days, so the team now ships 4-5 integrations per 2-week sprint. 14+ direct-API + 20+ browser-automation connectors (eClinicalWorks, Athenahealth, NextGen, ModMed, Nextech, Tebra, Dentrix Ascend, Kolla, CareStack, …).

Designed the EHR-agnostic abstraction so voice agents and FDE systems call one standardized API (cascade-search-patient, find-free-slots, post-appointment, get-changes-since, eligibility, refill) regardless of the underlying vendor — with self-serve clinic onboarding via the CareOS dashboard for operatory schedules, sync filters, event types, and free-slot logic.

Owned reliability + cost engineering at 3x scale: hash-based change-detection to cut EHR API cost under rate limits, idempotent writes with exponential backoff + partial-sync recovery + HITL review queue. Removed a scaling ceiling at ~30 clinics by re-architecting the compute topology — split background EHR-sync from the real-time voice-AI request path into independently-scaling GCP Cloud Run services (eliminated 5-10s deploy downtimes + resource contention; keyless CI/CD via Workload Identity Federation). Now 90+ clinics, zero deploy downtime.

Leading the next-gen voice runtime evaluation (Pipecat vs LiveKit) to replace Retell for larger clients — built end-to-end STT+LLM+TTS pipelines in both (Deepgram · LiteLLM-routed OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini · ElevenLabs · Upstash semantic cache); authored the multi-phase replacement plan (6 ADRs, risk matrix, target ≤2,500 tokens/call vs ~8,500-10,000 baseline, <800ms P50 voice-to-voice latency). Key design: Redis as source-of-truth + Jinja2 prompts rendered from frozen state each turn — constant token usage regardless of call length.

Additionally: designed Confido's senior-engineer technical interview framework (6 problems, 75-min phased-refactor format testing structured LLM-tool discipline; multiple internal appraisals); contributed across the Go/Temporal voice-agent backend and the Next.js clinician dashboard (SSE streaming transcripts, Clerk multi-tenant RBAC).

work.name: Nova Benefits Pvt. Ltd.
work.summary: Led one of three engineering teams (5-8 developers + a dedicated QA); collaborated with PM/CTO on roadmap. Owned the endorsement + onboarding modules of Nova's platform (250K active employees + 3K HR admins).
highlights: Consolidated dispersed endorsement + onboarding logic into a unified central module — 90% reduction in legit issues reported in core modules
Built a scalable HRMS integration framework — any new HRMS platform integrable in 3-4 days (Google Workspace, Zoho People, Darwinbox, etc.)
Integrated ICICI + CARE insurer APIs for automated endorsement data submission and batch tracking — reduced endorsement TAT from 14 days to a few hours
Led one of three engineering teams; managed 5-8 developers + a dedicated QA; collaborated with PM and CTO on requirements + roadmap alignment
Owned endorsements module for 250,000 active employees + 3,000 HR admins (insertion/deletion of employees to group + retail policies)
Integrated Razorpay SDK for standard web checkout on retail product purchases
Built additional platform modules: self-served mental/financial health assessment, CD Account management (client wallet), rater-based premium calculation + refund framework
Integrated Mailmodo for email automation and WATI for WhatsApp communications across CX surfaces

work.name: YouGov Research Pvt. Ltd.
work.summary: Owned end-to-end development, maintenance, and operations for YouGov.Chat — a chat-based user engagement platform with a 1,200,000-user panel — and the in-house CMS that powers content across YouGov platforms.
highlights: Built and maintained YouGov.Chat — chat-based engagement platform with a 1.2M-user panel — owning enhancements + complex problem-solving as IC
Built and maintained the in-house CMS providing a standard way of creating, editing, and publishing content across YouGov platforms
Set up Feature Flags via Unleash, enabling controlled rollouts of new platform features
Integrated AWS Cognito for social login across the panel
Integrated StoryBlok as a headless CMS for content management
Built user comments + rating with AWS Comprehend for opinion analysis + foul-language filtering
Integrated a bank API for automated reward distribution to panel users
Set up the platform's infrastructure via Terraform and implemented push notifications

work.name: Cimpress Technology Pvt. Ltd.
work.summary: Primary contributor on Cimpress's embroidery product (StitchX) — an ML / computer-vision pipeline converting customer artwork into machine-specific embroidery instructions. Promoted to Senior within 8 months; subsequently moved to a US-based team building a higher-vertical artwork design-editor product.
highlights: Re-architected StitchX's compute-heavy ML image-processing core (artwork → stitch-vector conversion: vectorization, stitch-path planning, color segmentation) to run client-side in the browser via WebAssembly + an async WebWorkers thread pool — cut conversion latency from ~4-5s server round-trip to <800ms and eliminated the dedicated high-compute server fleet (~60% infra cost reduction), enabling real-time interactive preview for production artists
Owned the StitchX image-processing / computer-vision pipeline end-to-end — requirement-gathering with production artists, feasibility analysis, and delivery
Promoted to Senior Software Developer within 8 months on project impact; subsequently moved to a US-based team on a higher-vertical artwork design-editor product
Designed a dedicated JMeter-based API load/functional testing project from scratch; maintained and upgraded unit/integration/UI test suites + CI/CD pipelines

work.name: Cimpress Technology Pvt. Ltd.
work.summary: Full-stack development on StitchX — Cimpress's ML-driven artwork-to-embroidery design tool. Entry point before promotion to Senior.
highlights: Built StitchX features applying image-processing / computer-vision techniques for artwork-to-stitch-vector transformation

work.name: Seclore Technology Pvt. Ltd.
work.summary: Full-stack engineer at a data-centric cyber-security organisation. Built back-end and front-end across Seclore's flagship Information Rights Management product, FileSecure — owning entire development lifecycle from requirements analysis to QA release.
highlights: Built back-end (server) + front-end for Seclore's flagship product FileSecure, owning the full development lifecycle from requirements analysis to QA release
Co-authored and maintained the OU Admin dashboard — monitored protected files, active users, and crucial data per Organizational Unit
Designed a centralized initialization + update mechanism for PolicyServer configuration — eliminated config-drift across distributed client deployments
Built installer + patch upload/validate/ready-to-fetch mechanism for product rollouts across all client deployments
Designed a License + privileges framework for end-user access control
Authored multiple responsive product pages + their backends; gained hands-on experience with many JavaScript frameworks + JQuery plug-ins

Includes custom skills (capture, research, distill-chat, query, triage-inbox, weekly-review), Karpathy-style atomic note pattern, Claude Code hooks + subagents integration.
